references.text:
[ErMa38] Erd\H{o}s, P\'{a}l and Mahler, Kurt, On the number of integers which can be represented by a binary form. Doc. Math. (2019), 475-481.
 
 [Wo15] Wooley, Trevor D., Sums of three cubes, II. Acta Arith. (2015), 73-100.

remarks.text: Mahler and Erd\H{o}s \cite{ErMa38} proved that $f_{k,2}(x) \gg x^{2/k}$. For $k=3$ the best known is due to Wooley \cite{Wo15},\[f_{3,3}(x) \gg x^{0.917\cdots}.\]This problem has been formalised in Lean as part of the Google DeepMind Formal Conjectures project.

statement.text: Let $k\geq 3$ and $f_{k,3}(x)$ denote the number of integers $\leq x$ which are the sum of three nonnegative $k$th powers. Is it true that\[f_{k,3}(x) \gg x^{3/k}\]or even $\gg_\epsilon x^{3/k-\epsilon}$?
